completed_tasks.name: Create backend venv, install packages, pre-download Whisper weights
Attempt FlashAttention 2 install and record the outcome
Write HTTPS probe server and HTTPS setup doc skeleton
Verify trusted HTTPS from the actual Android device
Build whisper_bench.py, reference transcript, and unit tests
Run the real Whisper benchmark on the builder recording
Build tts_ttfa.py, short reference transcript, and unit tests
Run the real TTS TTFA benchmark on the builder recording
Build llm_cancel.py and parser/idleness unit tests
Extend TTS probes so backend selection and labeling are explicit
Re-run labeled TTS measurements on OMEN-PC and persist the backend matrix
Update handoff/writeback inputs to cite backend-labeled numbers
Run warm-model TTS scenario matrix for F5, XTTS, LuxTTS, Chatterbox Turbo, TADA 1B, and Qwen3 on Windows and WSL

remaining_tasks.name: Implement shared TTS chunk planner in tts_scenario_matrix.py
Rerun Windows plus WSL TTS scenario matrix after chunking is implemented
Manual listening pass over tts_scenario_audio samples
Create structured manual quality sidecar keyed by runtime, engine, profile, and scenario
Decide whether LuxTTS should become a supported experimental engine after quality scoring
Optional: debug native Windows TADA 1B CUDA failure

blockers: Final long-form TTS comparisons are blocked on a shared chunk planner. XTTS long-form must be split below its 400-token streaming cap, and non-streaming engines must be benchmarked through chunked playback rather than raw whole-generation fallback.
Manual listening is now a hard gate for new TTS engines because the warm-model matrix found fast outputs that may be low quality.
User listening on 2026-04-23 found Chatterbox Turbo long samples are pure gibberish; treat Chatterbox long-form as rejected until reproduced or fixed.
Native Windows TADA 1B failed during warmup/generation with a CUDA error; WSL TADA rows are valid, but Windows TADA is not.
The current scenario harness uses fixed canned text scenarios, not a corpus of real RayMe LLM answers.

WSL access on 192.168.1.199 currently requires the pmpg Windows account; the rayme-ssh account reports no installed WSL distributions and remote WSL work should use direct `wsl -d Ubuntu -e sh -c ...` commands with fixed Linux paths.

human_actions_pending.action: Manual listening pass over generated TTS samples
human_actions_pending.context: Do this after the shared chunk planner is implemented and the Windows plus WSL matrix is rerun; latency alone is insufficient and the user already found Chatterbox Turbo long-form output is gibberish.

decisions.decision: Keep OMEN-PC as the only valid Phase 0 execution host and restore SSH from the repo-persisted key each session.
decisions.rationale: The earlier SEV-1 came from running backend work on the wrong machine and relying on disposable SSH state.

decisions.decision: Treat F5 and XTTS as not_applicable for attention backend labeling in the current probe.
decisions.rationale: Neither probe path exposes eager, SDPA, or FlashAttention selection; their runtime differences are streaming-path differences, not attention-backend switches.

decisions.decision: Treat Qwen3-TTS as an eager-attention baseline until flash_attn imports successfully on OMEN-PC.
decisions.rationale: The fresh labeled rerun and the FA2 install verdict both confirm that flash-attn is unavailable in this runtime.

decisions.decision: Use tts_attention_matrix.json as the source of truth for optimization backend labeling in future writeback.
decisions.rationale: The old tts_ttfa.json schema could be misread as an optimized Qwen result; the dedicated matrix makes the backend status explicit per engine.

decisions.decision: Use the pmpg Windows account for WSL-backed work on OMEN-PC; rayme-ssh remains valid for native Windows backend work.
decisions.rationale: Verified on 2026-04-22: pmpg sees Ubuntu on WSL2 and can run `wsl ls /`, while rayme-ssh reports no installed WSL distributions.

decisions.decision: For remote WSL work on OMEN-PC, prefer direct `wsl -d Ubuntu -e sh -c ...` commands with fixed Linux paths and avoid UNC WSL paths from the SSH session.
decisions.rationale: Verified on 2026-04-22: `/home/pmpg/rayme-wsl-probe` was created successfully inside Ubuntu, while `\\wsl.localhost\Ubuntu\...` and `\\wsl$\Ubuntu\...` were not reliably available from the non-interactive SSH session.

decisions.decision: Use `/home/pmpg/rayme/.venv-cu121` with `CUDA_HOME=/usr/local/cuda-12.1` as the reusable WSL accelerator env on OMEN-PC.
decisions.rationale: Verified on 2026-04-22: the env imports `torch 2.5.1+cu121` with CUDA visible on the RTX 3060 and also imports `deepspeed 0.18.9` successfully.

decisions.decision: Treat the upgraded OMEN-PC WSL env as both DeepSpeed-capable and FlashAttention-capable for future Linux-side probes.
decisions.rationale: Verified on 2026-04-23 after the in-place distro upgrade to Ubuntu 22.04.5 and glibc 2.35: `/home/pmpg/rayme/.venv-cu121` imports `torch 2.5.1+cu121`, `triton 3.1.0`, `deepspeed 0.18.9`, and `flash-attn 2.8.3` successfully on the RTX 3060.

decisions.decision: Keep F5-TTS as the current v1 default candidate while retaining XTTS as compatibility fallback.
decisions.rationale: No engine meets the target budget, and the latest OMEN-PC rerun still leaves F5 with the best TTFA while XTTS remains the only native streamer.

decisions.decision: Use the OMEN-PC WSL Docker Desktop Triton/TensorRT-LLM path as the current maximum-speed F5 runtime path, with a safe assembled model repo and CPU-preferred BLS response memory for the vocoder hop.
decisions.rationale: Verified on 2026-04-23: the stock prebuilt runtime image works after staging artifacts and applying the generated model repo patch that avoids the failing CUDA IPC output path under WSL.

decisions.decision: Do not replace the native Windows F5 short-response path with the current OMEN-PC WSL Triton path for v1 latency-sensitive acknowledgments.
decisions.rationale: Measured on 2026-04-23 with the same builder reference fixture and target text `Hey, got it.`: native Windows F5 median TTFA is 521.8 ms, while WSL Triton gRPC median TTFA is 1813.4 ms.

decisions.decision: Treat LuxTTS as the leading new experimental TTS candidate after warm-model scenario testing, but do not promote it until manual quality scoring is recorded.
decisions.rationale: Warm-model optimized results show LuxTTS winning short total time and medium/long total time, but manual listening found quality failures in the broader sample set and the harness does not yet score intelligibility or voice similarity.

decisions.decision: Treat Chatterbox Turbo as quality-blocked for long-form output.
decisions.rationale: The user listened to the generated samples on 2026-04-23 and reported that Chatterbox long samples are pure gibberish.

decisions.decision: Keep TADA 1B as WSL-only experimental until the native Windows CUDA failure is debugged.
decisions.rationale: WSL rows produced measurable samples, but the Windows path failed during warmup/generation with a CUDA error.

uncommitted_files: Worktree is dirty with broad planning-doc edits, generated Phase 0 artifacts, probe files, and spike files. Run `git status --short` before staging anything.
Do not commit with `git add .`; stage only the intended files after inspecting `git diff --cached --name-only` and `git diff --cached --stat`.
next_action: Implement the shared TTS chunk planner in `.planning/phases/00-measurement-gate/probes/tts_scenario_matrix.py`, then rerun `.planning/phases/00-measurement-gate/probes/tts_runtime_matrix_v2.py`. Manual listening and `MANUAL-QUALITY.csv` scoring come after regenerated chunked WAVs exist.
context_notes: 00-02 is now complete on the real Android device. The trusted HTTPS pass used Android Chrome against `https://192.168.1.199:8443` after installing the mkcert root CA on the phone; `window.isSecureContext === true`, `navigator.mediaDevices` was defined, and no certificate warning remained. The cert SANs covered both `rayme.local` and `192.168.1.199`, but the passing Phase 0 verification used the direct LAN IP because local hostname resolution was not configured. The temporary HTTP helper on port 8081 that was used only to transfer `mkcert-rootCA.crt` to the phone was removed immediately after verification. 00-07.1 is now complete for backend labeling only. The TTS probe and matrix explicitly label backend support on the current OMEN-PC native-Windows path: F5=not_applicable, XTTS=not_applicable, Qwen3=eager baseline with flash_attention_2 unavailable and sdpa not supported by the current probe. Fresh OMEN-PC rerun metrics are F5 517.3 ms / 0.388 RTF / 1990.2 MB, XTTS 534.3 ms / 0.59 RTF / 1897.6 MB, Qwen3 5626.8 ms / 2.705 RTF / 2509.1 MB. Remote unit tests for test_tts_ttfa.py now pass at 12/12 in the Phase 0 backend venv. `00-08` must cite the backend matrix for current-path claims and must not imply that WSL Python, WSL Triton long-form, XTTS DeepSpeed, or Qwen eager-vs-FA2 comparisons were already measured; that work is now tracked as `00-07.2`. On 192.168.1.199, WSL access is tied to the pmpg Windows account: `pmpg` sees `Ubuntu` on WSL2, SSH as `omen-pc\pmpg` now works via `C:\ProgramData\ssh\administrators_authorized_keys`, and `/home/pmpg/rayme-wsl-probe` was created successfully inside Ubuntu. Remote WSL commands should use direct `wsl -d Ubuntu -e sh -c ...` invocations with fixed Linux paths; UNC WSL paths were not reliable from the non-interactive SSH session. The WSL distro upgrade is complete: Ubuntu is now 22.04.5 with glibc 2.35, and the reusable WSL accelerator env `/home/pmpg/rayme/.venv-cu121` imports `torch 2.5.1+cu121`, `triton 3.1.0`, `deepspeed 0.18.9`, and `flash-attn 2.8.3` on the RTX 3060. `.planning/spikes/001-omen-pc-wsl-gpu-path/bootstrap-cu121-env.sh` now recreates that stack directly. The F5 Triton/TensorRT-LLM WSL path also now passes end to end: image `soar97/triton-f5-tts:24.12`, workspace `/home/pmpg/rayme/f5-triton-runtime/F5-TTS`, safe model repo `/home/pmpg/rayme/f5-triton-runtime/model_repo_cpuipc_18000`, host ports `18000/18001/18002`, and output `/home/pmpg/rayme/f5-triton-runtime/client_http_out.wav`. The stock prebuilt image still needs ad hoc `vocos` for stage 2 and `rjieba` for runtime launch, and the generated model repo copy must force the `vocoder` BLS response to CPU preferred memory to avoid the WSL CUDA IPC failure. Apples-to-apples short-response comparison for target text `Hey, got it.` is now captured in `.planning/spikes/002-f5-triton-trtllm-wsl-path/results/f5_short_ttfa_comparison.json`: native Windows F5 trials 524.5/520.1/521.8 ms (median 521.8 ms) versus WSL Triton gRPC trials 2801.2/1806.2/1813.4 ms (median 1813.4 ms), so the WSL Triton path is currently 3.475x slower for short acknowledgments. Hard safety rule: do not use `rm -rf` and do not delete files or directories through variable-expanded paths on any backend or WSL host.

incident.summary: Phase 0 execution previously ran on the wrong machine and lost SSH persistence, forcing repeated recovery loops.
incident.user_impact: Multiple days of avoidable churn and justified loss of trust.
prevention_rules: Use OMEN-PC only for Phase 0 backend work.
Restore SSH from .local/phase0-ssh/ each fresh session.
Verify hostname, whoami, python --version, and nvidia-smi before claiming backend access works.
Prefer the shortest validated recovery path.
Never run rm -rf on project or backend hosts.
Never delete files or directories through variable-expanded paths.

manual_quality_findings: User reported Chatterbox Turbo long samples are pure gibberish.
User reported some samples are subjectively atrocious.
Structured manual listening scores have not yet been recorded.
streaming_caveats: XTTS native streaming was measured for short_reply and medium_reply on Windows and WSL.
XTTS long_reply fell back to full-render timing because inference_stream hit the 400-token limit.
F5 optimized rows are simulated chunk streaming, not true incremental model streaming.
Qwen3 rows are simulated text streaming, not real audio streaming.
LuxTTS, Chatterbox Turbo, and TADA 1B rows are whole-generation raw baselines, not optimized chunked runtime paths.
Future benchmarks need a shared best-in-class chunk planner for all engines, including non-streaming engines.
required_harness_fixes: Implement token-aware XTTS long-text splitting so no inference_stream segment reaches the 400-token cap.

Implement shared long-form chunking for all engines, preferring sentence boundaries, enforcing engine-specific caps, avoiding tiny fragments, and measuring first-chunk TTFA, total stitched time, inter-chunk gaps, and stitched WAV quality.
Rerun the Windows plus WSL matrix after chunking is implemented before final long-form TTS decisions.
tts_scenario_matrix_v2.pause_note: Do not treat current XTTS long-form rows or non-streaming whole-generation rows as final long-form comparisons. Implement shared chunking first.
